restaurant_name: Crowne Plaza Atlanta Perimeter at Ravinia by IHG
blurb:
If you have out-of-town guests coming in and need a solid place to put them up near Perimeter, the Crowne Plaza at Ravinia is a reliable pick. It has an indoor pool, a gym, and a restaurant and bar on site, so they won't need to go far after a long travel day.

It's also worth knowing about if you're looking for a spot to grab a drink or a casual meal near the Perimeter area without a lot of fuss. The rating is strong for a hotel property, which usually means the basics are done right.
address: 4355 Ashford Dunwoody Rd, Atlanta, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
If you've been looking for a good biryani spot near Perimeter, this is the one locals keep coming back to. It's colorful, casual, and unfussy -- the kind of place where the food does the talking and nobody's trying to impress you with the decor. Good for a family dinner or a casual lunch when you want something with real flavor.

The biryani is obviously the main event here. It's served family-style, so it works well if you're bringing a group. The portions are solid for the price, and the traditional Indian dishes on the menu give you plenty of options beyond rice if you want to mix it up.

They open at 9am on weekends, which is a nice option if you want something different for a late breakfast or early lunch.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
Rreal Tacos at Sandy Springs has a 4.9 with nearly 10,000 reviews. That's not hype -- that's just people coming back and telling their friends. The place has good energy, the service is attentive, and it works for pretty much any occasion from a casual lunch to a late night out with friends. They're open until midnight most nights, which is handy around here.

Start with the Esquite if you want a flavor-packed opener. For drinks, reviewers rave about the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Rreal Passion Fruit pitcher -- both described as refreshing and well-balanced. Good call if you're coming with a group.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
A 4.9 with nearly 6,000 reviews is not an accident. Mister O1 has become a go-to for people around Sandy Springs who want real Italian thin-crust pizza, and it delivers. The vibe is relaxed enough for a weeknight dinner but good enough for when you have people visiting and want to take them somewhere you know won't let you down.

The pizza is the reason to come -- traditional thin crust with ingredients sourced from Italy or locally, depending on the item. The antipasti and salads are worth starting with before the pizza hits the table. It's the kind of spot where you end up back here a week later.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Ray's on the River is the place you take someone when you actually want to impress them. It sits right on the Chattahoochee, and the view alone makes it worth the drive to Powers Ferry. This is date-night territory, anniversary dinner territory, or the kind of place you bring family in town when you want to show off a little.

The Southern seafood is the reason to come. The menu leans into that Chattahoochee riverside feel, and the setting is intimate without being stuffy. With over 11,000 reviews at 4.6 stars, this place has been earning its reputation for a long time.

Heads up -- Sunday hours split into two seatings, with a break between 4pm and 5pm. Worth checking the website before you go if you're planning a Sunday evening.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
China Great Wall on Sandy Plains is exactly the kind of neighborhood Chinese spot you want to have nearby. It's a strip-mall place with no frills, but the food is solid and the menu covers all the familiar dishes you'd expect. Good for a quick family dinner or when you want reliable takeout without overthinking it.

The menu is straightforward and the portions are generous for the price. If you grew up eating American-Chinese takeout and just want something dependable, this is your place.

One thing to know: they don't open until noon on Saturdays and don't open until 3pm on Sundays, so plan accordingly if you're thinking about a weekend lunch.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd #7836, Marietta, GA 30066, USA

blurb:
Pacific Buffet on Chastain Meadows Parkway is the kind of place that's great when you have a group with different tastes and nobody wants to argue about where to eat. All-you-can-eat sushi, tempura, and American options under one roof means everyone finds something. Over 4,000 reviews and a 4.1 rating tells you people keep coming back.

The sushi and tempura are the main reasons to go. It's not a destination meal, but for a casual family dinner or a quick weeknight option where you want variety without a big bill, it hits the spot.

Pricing is easy on the wallet, and they're open every day through the week.
address: 2475 Chastain Meadows Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30066, USA

blurb:
Melt Yard on Whitlock is the kind of place that's more fun than you expect when you walk in. The vibe is great, the space is decorated with real personality, and it works well for a casual date night or hanging out with friends after work. The bar area is a solid spot to grab a seat.

The burgers are what people come back for, and if someone in your group needs a gluten-free bun that's not a problem here. Drinks are good too. It's not a fancy meal, but it's a really enjoyable one.

They have a back deck that's worth checking out when the weather cooperates. Friday and Saturday they stay open until 10pm if you're looking for a later dinner option.
address: 800 Whitlock Ave NW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA

blurb:
Hamp and Harry's on Roswell Street is the kind of spot that makes you feel like you found something special. The ambiance is warm and the food matches it. Reviewers consistently mention the presentation and the way the staff treats you, even when the place is packed. It's a great pick for a date night or a dinner where you actually want to enjoy the experience, not just the food.

People rave about the food across the board, and the service gets called out specifically in a lot of reviews. This is a place where the details matter and they seem to get it right consistently.

Just note they're closed on Mondays. Friday and Saturday they're open until 2am, which makes it a solid late-night option on the Square too.
address: 168 Roswell St NE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
If you haven't been to Brazilian Bakery Cafe on Powers Ferry, you're missing out on one of the better kept secrets in the area. It's a cheerful little spot that feels nothing like a typical American coffee shop. Good for a morning stop, a lazy weekend afternoon, or anytime you want something a little different.

The Brazilian light bites are the draw, and the traditional cakes and cookies are worth making a trip for on their own. Over 2,200 people have reviewed this place and it sits at 4.5 stars, which tells you the regulars are loyal.

They're open until 10pm every night, which is handy if you want a late treat without driving far.
address: 1260 Powers Ferry Rd A, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
